what_it_does: Acceptance test for vector_index.index_staleness: it seeds a vector index with a deterministic stub embedder over a small code:// / ui:// corpus, then verifies read-only staleness detection for identical, added, changed, dropped, and malformed corpus states.
observations: Seeds the index through vector_index.build_index with a stub embedder, not by hand-writing vectors.
Compares persisted content_hash values against the current corpus to decide fresh/missing/changed/extra.
Uses a temporary FsStore and sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN for hermetic execution.
Checks the full staleness report: fresh boolean, missing/changed/extra lists, and counts block.
Asserts malformed corpus items raise ValueError rather than being silently skipped.
Asserts the persisted index is unchanged after all staleness checks (read-only).
standouts: Hermetic seeding via build_index makes the persisted content_hash align with the check by construction.
Explicit fail-loud contract for malformed corpus items.
Verifies read-only behavior as a first-class property, not just correctness of staleness categories.
conventions: Follows the conv_index_acceptance pattern with a labeled check(label, cond) helper and a PASS counter.
Uses code:// and ui:// address grammar for corpus items.
Uses tempfile, sys.path insertion, and a finally block for cleanup.
Docstring enumerates numbered proofs and cross-references STATE.md:47.
concerns: Relies on a global mutable PASS counter.
A single early assertion failure prevents the remaining proofs from running in one invocation.
The stub embedder silently returns a default vector for unknown inputs, which is fine for seeding but could mask fixture drift if reused for other tests.
notes: Hermetic acceptance test that proves vector_index.index_staleness detects stale corpus states without embedding, network calls, or mutating the persisted index.
questions: What is the exact signature and return shape of vector_index.index_staleness?
Where is content_hash implemented and does build_index persist it per address?
Does index_addresses return only address strings or richer metadata?
Is the model= parameter accepted but ignored, or passed through to some other layer?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches; the file executes each proof claimed in the docstring.
intention_signals: Provide a cheap, no-network staleness probe so semantic retrieval does not silently run over a corpus that has grown or changed since the index was built.
novelty: Treats read-only-ness and fail-loud malformed-item handling as testable acceptance properties, and seeds the index through build_index to make content_hash alignment hold by construction.
contribution: A concrete acceptance-test fixture and scenario matrix for index_staleness, capturing expected behavior for fresh, missing, changed, extra, malformed, and read-only cases.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for vector_index.index_staleness that seeds a hermetic vector index and verifies read-only staleness detection across identical, added, changed, dropped, and malformed corpus states.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that index_staleness can detect corpus drift without embedding, network calls, or mutating the persisted index.

symbols.does: Inline assertion helper that counts passing checks and fails with a labeled message.
symbols.description: A tiny test helper used throughout the file. It asserts the supplied condition, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an 'ok <label>' line; on failure it raises AssertionError with the label.

symbols.does: Deterministic stub embedder that returns fixed 3-D vectors for known fixture texts.
symbols.description: Hermetic embedding function used only to seed the index through build_index. It maps a few fixture strings to fixed vectors and ignores transport, model, dim, and keyword arguments, ensuring the test needs no live model or :8001 service.
